channel.title: Integration on Plakar | The Open Standard for Backup and Restore
channel.description: Recent content in Integration on Plakar | The Open Standard for Backup and Restore

item.title: Backing up PostgreSQL with Plakar
item.description: We built a PostgreSQL integration for Plakar that covers both logical backups (pg_dump / pg_dumpall) and physical backups (pg_basebackup), making database backups as straightforward as any other Plakar backup: no scripts, no glue code.

item.title: Backing up Proxmox with Plakar: a third-party integration built in a few days
item.description: The team at FactorFX built a Proxmox integration for Plakar that wraps Proxmox’s native vzdump backups and stores them as deduplicated Plakar snapshots, making VM and container backups portable, encrypted, and easy to restore across clusters.

item.title: Backing up kubernetes clusters with Plakar
item.description: We built a Kubernetes integration for Plakar that backs up clusters at three levels: etcd (disaster recovery), manifests (granular restore and inspection), and persistent volumes (via CSI snapshots). This enables full cluster recovery, fine-grained restores, and data portability across environments.

item.title: Storing backups in an OCI registry
item.description: After a podcast discussion, we implemented an OCI registry storage backend. This article discusses the concept and showcases our proof of concept.
